body.p: Software is firmly embedded in all areas of our lives. Even if you don't use a "classic" computer and a smartphone yourself: the offices we have to go to regularly. When we go shopping. The surveillance cameras at train stations or on the road. The cars we drive. If only we talk to people who have "smart" devices with them. Everyone comes into contact with software somewhere.

Why this is a problem, but it doesn't have to be one. How one can protect oneself from this, at least to a certain extent, is what Erik G. (Co-coordinator of the FSFE Group in Berlin) will talk about.
